pastEvents.title: Climate Adaptation Sensors Workshop
pastEvents.summary: Hands-on session on designing sensor-based monitoring workflows for climate adaptation planning.
highlights: End-to-end workflow: from deployment to analytics
Calibration basics for low-cost sensor networks
Data quality checks and uncertainty awareness
pastEvents.details: This workshop brought together researchers and practitioners to discuss practical monitoring systems for climate adaptation. Participants worked through a reference workflow covering sensor selection, deployment planning, calibration/validation, and downstream analytics. We also emphasized data quality checks, uncertainty communication, and reproducible documentation for field-to-model pipelines.

pastEvents.title: GeoAI for Early Warning: From Satellites to Alerts
pastEvents.summary: A talk on building GeoAI pipelines that turn remote sensing signals into actionable early warning outputs.
highlights: Feature extraction for drought and stress indicators
Model evaluation strategies under data gaps
How to design alert thresholds responsibly
pastEvents.details: The webinar covered end-to-end pipeline design for translating satellite-derived indicators into early warning signals. Speakers discussed robust evaluation when training data is limited, techniques for handling missingness, and practical considerations for choosing alert thresholds that are explainable to stakeholders.

pastEvents.title: Water & Hydrology Field Methods Seminar
pastEvents.summary: Methods seminar focused on river monitoring, hydrology mapping, and field-data alignment for GIS models.
highlights: Aligning field measurements with GIS rasters
Common pitfalls in hydrology dataset integration
pastEvents.details: This seminar focused on practical field methods and how to align measurement outputs with GIS workflows. Attendees learned sampling strategies, how to maintain reliable metadata, and ways to reconcile measurement scales between field instruments and raster datasets. We also discussed integration pitfalls and checklists for ensuring traceability.

upcomingEvents.title: SCD Open Lab Day: Demo of Climate Monitoring Pipelines
upcomingEvents.summary: Come see live demos of sensor-to-dashboard monitoring pipelines and ask questions.
upcomingEvents.details: The Open Lab Day will feature guided demonstrations of SCD monitoring pipelines, including data ingestion from sensor networks, automated quality checks, and dashboard-ready analytics. Visitors can interact with the team, understand dataset organization practices, and get guidance on potential collaboration topics.

upcomingEvents.title: Biodiversity Mapping Using Multi-Temporal Imagery
upcomingEvents.summary: A practical training on mapping biodiversity indicators using multi-temporal satellite data.
upcomingEvents.details: This training session is designed for researchers and students who want to apply multi-temporal imagery workflows. We will cover preprocessing, change detection concepts, and indicator extraction. The session concludes with practical tips for structuring reproducible experiments.

upcomingEvents.title: SCD Research Roundtable: Water Security & Decision Support
upcomingEvents.summary: Discussion roundtable to align research outputs with water security decision needs.
upcomingEvents.details: The roundtable will connect researchers working on hydrology, remote sensing, and decision-support systems. We will discuss how to translate evidence into decision-ready outputs and how to design evaluation methods that match stakeholder requirements.

upcomingEvents.title: Student Poster Session: Climate Informatics Showcase
upcomingEvents.summary: A showcase of student projects in climate informatics, sensors, and GeoAI.
upcomingEvents.details: Students will present poster projects in climate informatics, sensor-based monitoring, and GeoAI applications. This session encourages constructive peer feedback and supports early-stage collaborations.
